popularStory.headline: Richard Treat
popularStory.description:
Wikipedia Article: http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Richard_Treat

Richard Treat (or Trott) (1584–1669) was an early settler in New England and a patentee of the Royal Charter of Connecticut, 1662.

zh.lifeSummary: Richard Treat Sr.是Robert Treat和Honour Lambert的兒子，於1584年8月28日出生在英國薩默塞特比特明斯特。他於1615年4月26日與Alice Gaylord在英國薩默塞特比特明斯特結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和7個女兒的父母。他於1637年移民到布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼素福爾可貝林罕維瑟斯費爾德。他去世於1669年2月14日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州哈特福德維瑟斯費爾德享年84歲，埋葬在美國康乃狄克州哈特福德維瑟斯費爾德維利亞 其特利。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Richard Treat Sr.是Robert Treat和Honour Lambert的儿子，于1584年8月28日出生在英国薩默塞特比特明斯特。他于1615年4月26日与Alice Gaylord在英国薩默塞特比特明斯特结婚。他们至少是6个儿子和7个女儿的父母。他于1637年移民到布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼素福爾可貝林罕維瑟斯費爾德。他去世于1669年2月14日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州哈特福德維瑟斯費爾德享年84岁，埋葬在美国康涅狄格州哈特福德維瑟斯費爾德維利亞 其特利。

nameMeanings.name: Treat
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Americanized form of German   Tritt   or   Trede  .

variants.name: Tritt
meanings: German: apparently from Old High German tretan ‘to step’. However, a South German family of this name are descendants of late 16th-century immigrants from Como, Italy, who bore the name de Triddi.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: Richard Treat Sr. was born on 28 August 1584, in Pitminster, Somerset, England as the son of Robert Treat and Honour Lambert. He married Alice Gaylord on 26 April 1615, in Pitminster, Somerset, England. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 7 daughters. He immigrated to Wethersfield, Bellingham, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America in 1637. He died on 14 February 1669, in Wethersfield, Hartford, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America, at the age of 84, and was buried in Village Cemetery, Wethersfield, Hartford, Connecticut, United States.

fr.lifeSummary: Richard Treat Sr. est né le 28 août 1584, à Pitminster, Somerset, Angleterre, fils de Robert Treat et Honour Lambert. Il a épousé Alice Gaylord le 26 avril 1615, à Pitminster, Somerset, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils et 7 filles. Il a immigré au Wethersfield, Bellingham, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique en 1637. Il est décédé le 14 février 1669, à Wethersfield, Hartford, Connecticut Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique, à l’âge de 84 ans, et a été enterré à Village Cemetery, Wethersfield, Hartford, Connecticut, États-Unis.

de.lifeSummary: Richard Treat Sr. wurde am 28. August 1584 in Pitminster, Somerset, England, als Sohn von Robert Treat und Honour Lambert geboren. Er hat am 26. April 1615 in Pitminster, Somerset, England Alice Gaylord geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 6 Söhne und 7 Töchter. Er ist 1637 nach Wethersfield, Bellingham, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America eingewandert. Er starb am 14. Februar 1669 im Alter von 84 Jahren in Wethersfield, Hartford, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America und wurde in Village Cemetery, Wethersfield, Hartford, Connecticut, Vereinigte Staaten begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Richard Treat Sr. è nato il il 28 agosto 1584 nel Pitminster, Somerset, England come figlio di Robert Treat e Honour Lambert. Lui sposato Alice Gaylord il 26 aprile 1615, nel Pitminster, Somerset, England. Erano genitori di almeno 6 figli e 7 figlie. Immigrato nel Wethersfield, Bellingham, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America nel 1637. Morì il 14 febbraio 1669, nel Wethersfield, Hartford, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America, all'età di 84 anni, e fu sepolto a Village Cemetery, Wethersfield, Hartford, Connecticut, Stati Uniti d'America.

ja.lifeSummary: Richard Treat Sr.は1584年8月28日にイギリスソメルセトピトミンスターでRobert TreatとHonour Lambertの息子として生まれた。1615年4月26日にイギリスソメルセトピトミンスターでAlice Gaylordと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と7人の娘の親であった。彼は1637年にイギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニースフォルクベリンガムウエスエルスフィールドに移住した。彼は1669年2月14日にイギリス植民地アメリカコネチカット州ハートフォードウエスエルスフィールドで84歳で死去し，アメリカ合衆国コネチカット州ハートフォードウエスエルスフィールドヴィラッジ セメテリーにて埋葬された。

ko.Wethersfield Village Cemetery, Wethersfi: 미국 코네티컷 주 하트퍼드 베더스필드 비라지 세멧리
ko.lifeSummary: Richard Treat Sr.(은)는 1584년 8월 28일 영국 서머세트 피트민스터에서 Robert Treat(와)과 Honour Lambert의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 1615년 4월 26일 영국 서머세트 피트민스터에서 Alice Gaylord(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 일곱 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1637년에 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 수폴크 벨링함 w더스필드로 이민을 떠났다. 그는 1669년 2월 14일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 코네티컷 주 하트퍼드 베더스필드에서 84세의 나이로 사망했으며 미국 코네티컷 주 하트퍼드 베더스필드 비라지 세멧리에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Richard Treat Sr. nasceu em 28 de agosto de 1584, em Pitminster, Somerset, Inglaterra filho de Robert Treat e Honour Lambert. Ele casou-se com Alice Gaylord em 26 de abril de 1615, em Pitminster, Somerset, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos e 7 filhas. Ele imigrou para o Wethersfield, Bellingham, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America em 1637. Ele faleceu em 14 de fevereiro de 1669, em Wethersfield, Hartford, Conecticute, British Colonial America, com 84 anos, e foi sepultado em Village Cemetery, Wethersfield, Hartford, Conecticute, Estados Unidos da América.

ru.lifeSummary: Richard Treat Sr. родился 28 августа 1584 г. в местности питминстир, сомирсет, Англия. Его родителей звали Robert Treat и Honour Lambert. Он женился на женщине по имени Alice Gaylord 26 апреля 1615 г. в местности питминстир, сомирсет, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 7 дочерей. Он иммигрировал в ветирсфиелд, беллингхам, суффолк, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика в 1637 году. Он умер 14 февраля 1669 г. в местности ветирсфиелд, хартфорд, Коннектикут, бритиш колоняль амирика в возрасте 84 лет и был похоронен в местности вилладж кеметири, ветирсфиелд, хартфорд, Коннектикут, Соединенные штаты.
